title: Database Normalization
summary: Eliminate data redundancy and anomalies by organizing tables into normal forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF).
content_html:
Normalization is the process of structuring a database to reduce redundancy and improve data integrity.

Update Anomalies (What Normalization Prevents)

  • Insertion anomaly: can't add data without unrelated data
  • Deletion anomaly: deleting a row loses unintended data
  • Update anomaly: same data in multiple rows must be updated everywhere

First Normal Form (1NF)

Each cell contains an atomic (single, indivisible) value; no repeating groups:

  • No multi-valued attributes (e.g., no 'phone1, phone2' in one cell)
  • Each row is uniquely identifiable (has a primary key)

Second Normal Form (2NF)

Must be in 1NF AND every non-key attribute must fully depend on the entire primary key (no partial dependencies):

  • Only applies to tables with composite (multi-column) primary keys
  • Move partial dependencies to a separate table

Third Normal Form (3NF)

Must be in 2NF AND no non-key attribute depends on another non-key attribute (no transitive dependencies):

  • If A → B and B → C, move B → C to a separate table

Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)

Stricter than 3NF: every determinant must be a candidate key.

exercises.title: 1NF requirement
options: Atomic values in each cell and a primary key
exercises.answer: Atomic values in each cell and a primary key

exercises.title: 2NF requirement
options: Partial dependencies on a composite primary key
exercises.answer: Partial dependencies on a composite primary key

exercises.title: Update anomaly
options: The same data is stored in multiple rows and must be updated everywhere
exercises.answer: The same data is stored in multiple rows and must be updated everywhere

exercises.title: Transitive dependency
exercises.prompt: If StudentID → Department and Department → Dean, there is:

exercises.title: Atomic value
options: Storing 'red, blue, green' in one 'colors' cell
exercises.answer: Storing 'red, blue, green' in one 'colors' cell

exercises.title: 2NF applies to
options: The primary key is composite (multiple columns)
The table has more than 5 columns
exercises.answer: The primary key is composite (multiple columns)

exercises.title: Normalization benefit
exercises.prompt: The main benefit of normalization is:
options: Reduced data redundancy and improved integrity
exercises.answer: Reduced data redundancy and improved integrity

exercises.title: Deletion anomaly
options: Deleting one record unintentionally removes other needed information
exercises.answer: Deleting one record unintentionally removes other needed information

exercises.title: BCNF vs 3NF
options: Stricter than 3NF — every determinant must be a candidate key
exercises.answer: Stricter than 3NF — every determinant must be a candidate key
